food.name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil

identity.basisReason: Per-100g as-sold basis matches oil labeling and avoids recipe dilution.

scoreReadiness.summary: Core oil nutrition is clean enough for launch scripting, but the entry still treats quality and polyphenol strength generically rather than tying them to a specific bottle or harvest profile.
blockers: polyphenol-rich reputation is real but not quantified in the current schema
authenticity and storage quality vary meaningfully across real-world extra virgin olive oils

sourceNotes: Identity locked to plain extra virgin olive oil, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with USDA FoodData Central style extra virgin olive oil entries.
Polyphenol/quality discussion is intentionally treated as context rather than a locked nutrient metric.

topPros.title: polyphenol reputation is a real plus
topPros.explanation: It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile.

topPros.title: contains useful polyphenols
topPros.explanation: Adds extra value beyond the core fat chemistry alone.

topPros.title: very simple ingredient identity
topPros.explanation: A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

topCons.title: extremely energy dense
topCons.explanation: Still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast.

topCons.title: quality varies a lot
topCons.explanation: Source and authenticity matter in practice.

topCons.title: easy to overpour without noticing
topCons.explanation: A healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are holding it back most. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 51.4. Section scores: fats 84.3, vitamins 56, minerals 0, pros 66.7, cons 50.

closing.summary: Extra Virgin Olive Oil has a genuinely strong overall case. The practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.

scenes.narrationText: Vitamin e at 93% daily value, vitamin k at 50% daily value stand out most. That gives this food real vitamin support.

scenes.narrationText: The upsides first: polyphenol reputation is a real plus. It brings a widely recognised antioxidant angle beyond the visible macro profile. Contains useful polyphenols. Adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. Very simple ingredient identity. A clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.
